action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13's base-model eval submission failed because checkpoints/base_model isn't a valid HF checkpoint directory (likely a broken symlink or missing in the isolated eval container). The merged model scores 0.29 on val but only 0.009 on test. A plausible root cause is that the fine-tuned model outputs absolute pixel coordinates directly (because training targets were raw pixels), while predict.py blindly rescales by image_width/1000. If ShowUI-desktop images are ~1000 px this would look okay on val, but ScreenSpot-Pro images are likely larger (e.g. 1920+), causing catastrophic coordinate scaling on test. Analysis: Before copying the base model or retraining, I must verify the coordinate scale of the merged model's raw outputs against ground-truth val labels. Decision: Write a quick diagnostic script that runs inference on a handful of val samples, prints raw token outputs vs. absolute GT, and checks image size distributions.
